metadata.source: CLUTRR v1 (Sinha et al., EMNLP 2019, arXiv:1908.06177). Pre-generated TEST splits of configs gen_train234_test2to10 and gen_train23_test2to10, staged in temp/datasets/ from the kliang5/CLUTRR_huggingface_dataset GitHub raw CSV mirror.
metadata.title: CLUTRR Crisp-Gold Calibration Anchor: Atomic + Multi-Hop Kinship Triples with k-Difficulty Splits
metadata.selection_rationale: CLUTRR is rule-based/templated so its kinship gold is exact (no annotation noise) — the property that lets it host the FDR calibration diagonal; proof_state yields crisp ATOMIC (directly-stated) + MULTI-HOP (inferred) triple gold for the pre-registered disconfirmation. Secondary candidate ProofWriter (kept in temp/datasets/) has only T/F/Unknown answers over rule/fact theories, not kinship atomic+multi-hop triples, so it is excluded (target_num_datasets=1).
metadata.row_is_example: Each CLUTRR story is one example; 190 stories -> 190 examples.
metadata.schema_note: input/output are JSON-serialized strings (parse with json.loads). input keys: doc_id, document_text (brackets stripped), document_text_bracketed, entities[{name,gender,type,node_index}], query{head,tail}. output keys: atomic_facts[{head,relation,tail}], multi_hop_facts[{head,relation,tail,derived_from,path_len,is_query_target}], multi_hop_query_target{head,relation,tail}, kinship_edge_graph{nodes,edges}.
metadata.example_ordering: round-robin across k (order [2, 10, 6, 3, 9, 7, 4, 8, 5]) so mini/preview span short and long chains
metadata.filtering: Restricted to canonical CLUTRR simple-path chains: distinct entities==k+1, distinct story edges==k, |atomic|==k, |multi_hop|==k-1, exactly one proof root equal to the query target, and a genders-order node->name map that reproduces the proof_state atomic leaves. Guarantees deterministic crisp gold.
metadata.out_of_scope: No decoy/entrapment generation, no LLM scoring, no FDR/precision/recall, no Prolog execution, no transitive-closure enrichment — those belong to the experiment artifact.

examples.input: {"doc_id": "b9e9666b-1371-400c-a8d0-1a9d3d4a0324", "document_text": "Dan's brother, Micheal, and his grandmother, Gabrielle, met him for his birthday dinner at the local diner.", "document_text_bracketed": "[Dan]'s brother, [Micheal], and his grandmother, [Gabrielle], met him for his birthday dinner at the local diner.", "entities": [{"name": "Gabrielle", "gender": "female", "type": "person", "node_index": 0}, {"name": "Dan", "gender": "male", "type": "person", "node_index": 1}, {"name": "Micheal", "gender": "male", "type": "person", "node_index": 2}], "query": {"head": "Gabrielle", "tail": "Micheal"}}
examples.metadata_noisy_story: [Dan]'s brother, [Micheal], and his grandmother, [Gabrielle], met him for his birthday dinner at the local diner.

examples.input: {"doc_id": "c5a0fb69-4629-45cc-ad52-c905d2fde01b", "document_text": "Victor invited his mother Joy and grandmother Debra over for dinner. Debra called her son Kenneth and his sister Melissa was at his house visiting so he said he would call her back later. Melissa and her son John made hot dogs. John's brother Scott ate one. Dale's nephew Scott was happy to hear that he will be arriving at the airport later tonight. Morgan and her husband Dale went to go ice skating with their daughter Danielle. Danielle's sister Ouida could not go because she has a broken leg.", "document_text_bracketed": "[Victor] invited his mother [Joy] and grandmother [Debra] over for dinner. examples.metadata_noisy_story: [Debra] called her son [Kenneth] and his sister [Melissa] was at his house visiting so he said he would call her back later. [Victor] invited his mother [Joy] and grandmother [Debra] over for dinner. [Dale]'s nephew [Scott] was happy to hear that he will be arriving at the airport later tonight. [Morgan] and her husband [Dale] went to go ice skating with their daughter [Danielle]. [Danielle]'s sister [Ouida] could not go because she has a broken leg. [Melissa] and her son [John] made hot dogs. [John]'s brother [Scott] ate one.

examples.input: {"doc_id": "42ae6a49-6451-40fb-8f74-feb29fcf40d3", "document_text": "Helen went to her son Leo's House Leo went to lunch with his aunt Kecia. Kecia and her husband Thomas took their son Matthew to the wedding. Matthew's brother Phillip hates weddings and did n't go. Phillip and his sister Laura went out for ice cream.", "document_text_bracketed": "[Helen] went to her son [Leo]'s House [Leo] went to lunch with his aunt [Kecia]. [Kecia] and her husband [Thomas] took their son [Matthew] to the wedding. [Matthew]'s brother [Phillip] hates weddings and did n't go.
